finding_results.evidence: Morning-routine openings varied: chapters/chapter_14.md:7 now opens 'The shower ran at 6:15' (environment lead), chapters/chapter_14.md:9 now opens 'Dark pants, a blouse she'd ironed' (object lead). Coffee-shop openings varied: chapters/chapter_14.md:35 now opens 'At the counter she ordered' (prepositional lead), chapters/chapter_14.md:41 now opens 'The last physical book she'd held' (object lead), chapters/chapter_14.md:43 now opens 'Back at the table' (location lead), chapters/chapter_14.md:47 now opens 'Between pages, her mind went to Dan' (prepositional lead), chapters/chapter_14.md:57 now opens 'The book drew her back' (object-as-subject lead), chapters/chapter_14.md:67 now opens 'The coffee shop smelled of more than beans' (environment lead). Surviving 'She/Mira [verb]' paragraph openings in lines 3-75: line 5, 11, 23, 25, 27, 31, 53, 75 — total 8. Maximum consecutive run is 3 (lines 23, 25, 27 — the deliberate left-turn sequence). Non-subject openings in morning-routine and coffee-shop sequences: 8.
finding_results.notes: Changed 8 paragraph openings from 'She [verb]' to varied non-subject constructions (environment, object, location, prepositional phrase). Preserved the deliberate anaphoric cluster at lines 23-27 (refusal, walking Theo, turning left) as the strongest candidate for intentional rhythm. The left-turn echo at lines 27/31 ('She turned left' / 'She walked left') is preserved as thematic anaphora with line 29 breaking consecutiveness.

finding_results.evidence: Surviving instances: chapters/chapter_14.md:25 ('the way he always did') and chapters/chapter_14.md:193 ('the way mothers had always known things') — 2 total. Replacements: chapters/chapter_14.md:65 changed from 'the way she'd offered' to 'as she'd offered'; chapters/chapter_14.md:133 changed from 'processing it the way he processed things — not strategically, the way she would' to 'taking it in — not strategically, as she would have' (restructured syntax); chapters/chapter_14.md:145 changed from 'the way you hear yourself recite a number' to 'rote, unbidden, like reciting a phone number she'd memorized against her will' (simile); chapters/chapter_14.md:151 changed from 'the way she dismissed' to 'the reflexive swat she gave' (metaphor); chapters/chapter_14.md:183 changed from 'the way a quarterly report was legible' to 'as a quarterly report was legible' (comparative 'as'); chapters/chapter_14.md:207 changed from 'the way the refrigerator's click was' to 'absorbed into the room's frequency' (direct statement).
finding_results.notes: Reduced from 8 total instances to 2. The finding identified 6 but the chapter contained 8 (lines 65, 151, and 183 were additional unflagged instances). All were addressed. The three replacements specified by the acceptance test (lines 133, 145, 207) use distinct grammatical shapes: restructured syntax with 'as' clause, simile with 'like', and direct statement respectively.

finding_results.evidence: Rectangle of light: chapters/chapter_14.md:63 ('The rectangle of sun on the floor') and chapters/chapter_14.md:111 ('a long rectangle on the floor') — 2 instances with distinct phrasing. Third instance at line 119 changed to 'the light moved off the floor'; fourth at line 117 changed to 'the light on the floor'. Refrigerator click: chapters/chapter_14.md:11 ('The refrigerator clicking its forty-minute click') and chapters/chapter_14.md:111 ('The refrigerator clicking') — 2 instances with distinct phrasing. Third instance at line 147 removed. 'Specific' as emphatic adjective modifying abstract noun: chapters/chapter_14.md:111 changed from 'specific stillness' to 'held-breath stillness'; chapters/chapter_14.md:151 changed from 'The specific, unperformable recognition' to 'The unperformable recognition'. Surviving 'specific' instances at lines 65 and 167 modify physical nouns (heat), not abstract nouns.
finding_results.notes: All three motifs brought to or below the 2-instance cap. Rectangle-of-light had 4 instances (finding identified 3; line 117 was an additional unflagged occurrence). Refrigerator click reduced from 3 to 2 by removing the line-147 instance. 'Specific' as emphatic modifier of abstract nouns reduced from 2 to 0; the two strongest 'specific' uses (line 65 modifying sensory 'heat', line 167 modifying physical 'heat') are retained as they target physical rather than abstract nouns and fall outside the acceptance test's scope.

summary: All three findings resolved. F14-02: Reduced 'She/Mira [verb]' paragraph openings from 16 to 8 in lines 3-75 by introducing environment, object, location, and prepositional leads in morning-routine and coffee-shop sequences. Maximum consecutive run is 3 (the deliberate left-turn sequence at lines 23-27). F14-03: Reduced 'the way [clause]' from 8 instances to 2, retaining lines 25 and 193 as the strongest deployments. Replacements use varied comparative constructions (restructured syntax, simile, metaphor, direct statement). F14-04: All three overused motifs capped at 2 instances each — rectangle-of-light (lines 63, 111), refrigerator-click (lines 11, 111), 'specific' + abstract noun (0 remaining; physical-noun uses preserved). Surviving pairs use distinct phrasing. No sanitization introduced; all revisions maintain or strengthen the chapter's prose texture without softening register or retreating from difficult material.
